characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The leader of theEnsemble Cast. Barney is an average family man trying to keep his sanity as he runs the 12th Precinct, a task made difficult both by criminals and his own officers. He emphasizes using compassion and perspective in dealing with the public, occasionally to the frustration of his squad. His signature method of dealing with crime is having the people involved talk it out until one of them decides to drop charges, as most arrestees are ordinary citizens who just need a chance to calm down. Actor Allusion: Ironically, Hal Linden was born in New York.Barney Millersets in New York in the 1970s-80s. Beleaguered Bureaucrat: On occasion. Beware the Nice Ones: Barney prefers to sort out conflicts with perspective and discussion, butObstructive Bureaucratsand others have pushed him past the breaking point several times to a verbalexplosion(Harris did too, in "Uniform Day" from 1980, wherein Harris didn't want to wear the NYPD-standard blue uniform that everyone in the department had to wear once a year, and he and Barney had a heated discussion about it that came to verbal blows, with Barney having the last word, and laying it down: either comply with the regulation that day, or start looking for another job). Bothering by the Book: In "Voice Analyzer", after Wojo sends the supposed lie detector into overdrive when asked his name and Dietrich utterly confounds it with a series ofBlatant Lies, Barney decidesnotto submit his voice to analysis to prove his point to Scanlan. It is, after all, a "voluntary" evaluation, and the refusal of the captain to do it says a lot.

It is, after all, a "voluntary" evaluation, and the refusal of the captain to do it says a lot.

characters.name: Other Officers
characters.description: A Puerto Rican officer who was good at his job and rarely got on anyone's nerves (as the other officers were prone to doing). He was a regular cast member for the first two seasons, but Sierra left the show in the third. Emotional Language Shift: Speaks in rapid-fire Spanish when he gets excited orangry. Heroic BSoD: After he shoots and kills a robber threatening hostages. Motor Mouth: He starts talking in really fast Spanish when he's agitated. Put on a Bus: He left early in the show, but it's not much acknowledged until several seasons later when his (short-lived) replacement is sent. Ah, bureaucracy.

characters.name: Recurring Visitors
characters.description: The proprietor of Cotterman's Liquors and a "regular customer" of the 12th. He has been robbed a truly impressive number of times. Everyone Has Standards: In "Identity", Calvin Kendall, an informant who is in the witness protection program, is about to face prison time and potential endangerment of his life. Cotterman is ready to have him sent away to prison; in the end, he mentions that he had revenge in his heart, and he made the decision not to send Kendall to prison so that he could cut back on revenge. Expy: In the first season, there was a Jewish shop owner who called himself a repeat customer, only he was played by a different actor and ran a deli instead. Marathon Running: Cable channel TV Land once ran a marathon of his episodes called "Welcome Back, Cotterman". Mauve Shirt:He's fatally shot in "Homicide" after Barney says they can't help him set up a sting because the squad only deals with homicide cases now, even though the 12th is very near his liquor store. After his disappointment at being turned away by the 12th and being referred to another precinct, Cotterman tries to deal with it himself and gets killed.
